div1.entryFree: Suborn, to procure by secret collusion, to abet: thou hast --ed the goldsmith to arrest me, Err. IV, 4, 85. what peer hath been --ed to grate on you? H4B IV, 1, 90. whom I did s. to do this ruthless piece of butchery, R3 IV, 3, 4. they (the murderers of Duncan) were --ed, Mcb. II, 4, 24. Applied especially to false witnesses: Sonn. 125, 13. Meas. V, 106. Meas. V, 106 H6A V, 4, 21. H6B III, 1, 180. Oth. III, 4, 153.
